title: Add capability dependencies to native Codex planning
summary: Change native Codex planning and native Codex planning so one primary project task can explicitly depend on usable skills from another known project, with ordered dependency steps, write plans, artifact promotion, and provenance while keeping the dependency project isolated.
acceptance_criteria: the PRD defines capability dependencies as a primary-task feature, not as direct project wrapper or connection-registry integration
native Codex planning can create one unified primary project task that explicitly records external project workflow capabilities it is allowed to call
task JSON supports capability_dependencies for external project workflow capabilities that a unified primary task may call during execution
task JSON supports ordered dependency_steps with purpose, dependency project, selected skill, expected inputs, expected outputs, allowed writes, protected paths, and provenance requirements
capability_dependencies can be provided explicitly or inferred from natural language, but inferred dependencies must be confirmed with the operator before task creation
capability dependency suggestions come only from known registered project trackers and existing project, task, skill, or artifact records, not from broad live scans or project self-advertising

when a dependency project is selected, native Codex planning loads all usable known skills or skill metadata for that project plus enough relationship context to understand how selected skills can be called, without auditing the entire workflow agent
task drafts with capability_dependencies include a compact selected-skill map showing intended purpose, call boundary, expected artifacts, allowed writes, and protected paths
loaded-but-not-selected dependency skills remain outside the approved call surface
selected dependency skills may call documented helper skills as part of their normal workflow, but helper usage is recorded in provenance after use
dependency calls during intake or planning may create real artifacts when needed to understand or prepare the task
intake or planning dependency artifacts are labeled with phase, primary task provenance, dependency project, source workflow or skill, purpose, generated files, and protected-boundary metadata
post-planning PRD inclusion promotes intake or planning dependency artifacts into official task artifacts while preserving original provenance and adding promotion metadata
intake or planning dependency artifacts not named in the PRD remain linked evidence or context, not final deliverables
dependency-created artifacts are indexed under the primary project task by default and do not mutate the dependency project tracker with backlinks
native Codex planning may propose a new dependency_step mid-execution only after loading the referenced workflow context, updating provenance and write boundaries, and receiving operator approval

before implementation starts, PRD or issue artifacts include a dependency_write_plan for every dependency_step naming expected output paths or patterns, allowed write zones, protected paths, artifact promotion rules, provenance requirements, and stop conditions
implementation preflight verifies every dependency_step has an approved dependency_write_plan and blocks dependency execution when any required plan is missing or incomplete
if a selected dependency skill or helper tries to write outside the approved artifact boundary, execution stops and asks for operator approval

after a dependency_step runs, the task records provenance with primary project, primary task id, dependency step id, dependency project, selected skill, helper skills used, inputs, generated artifacts, dependency_write_plan used, phase, timestamp, and artifact status
the derived task flow preserves native Codex planning preload, native Codex planning resume behavior, phase guard, previous workflow phase handling, and ECC concepts

validation or fixtures prove only approved dependency skills are callable, source-project control-plane files remain unchanged, dependency_write_plan preflight blocks incomplete steps, and provenance is recorded for dependency-created artifacts

context_snapshot.summary: Load workflow-foundry state, every non-done workflow-foundry task, previous workflow/ECC references, and native Codex planning before shaping the one-way project skill connection workflow.

session_log.event: Created intake task after user invoked native Codex planning for a one-way project skill connection workflow where the foundry can use approved project skills while the source project remains isolated.
Moved to planning after user asked to grill the one-way project skill connection workflow.

Resolved first boundary: connected project skill source files are read-only for the foundry; the foundry may run approved skills, but any files or records produced by a foundry run must be stored and labeled as foundry-owned rather than as source-project testing or usage artifacts.

Resolved foundry-owned run storage: connected project skill run records should live under projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/connected-runs/<source-project-slug>/<run-id>/, separate from source-project testing sessions or project-owned usage artifacts.

Resolved discovery mode: connected projects should expose all skill paths to the foundry by default, but integration must compare the source project against the foundry so skills are connected intentionally rather than through a narrow allowlist or blind import.

Resolved comparison output: the foundry-owned integration report should classify every discovered source skill as run-as-is, needs-foundry-wrapper, duplicates-foundry-skill, conflicts-with-foundry-rule, or do-not-run.

Resolved integration approval gate: source project connection should be rare and explicit; the foundry must not connect a project or create a foundry-owned wrapper or adapter unless the operator deliberately approves that integration.

Resolved connection record granularity: create one foundry-owned connection record for the whole source project, and record inside it which discovered skills are connected or not connected along with their comparison classification.

Resolved connection storage: keep project-level connection records under projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/connections/<source-project-slug>.json, separate from per-run outputs under projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/connected-runs/<source-project-slug>/<run-id>/.

Resolved operator entrypoint: keep native Codex planning as the primary user-facing command, and activate connection-specific behavior only when the operator explicitly says the initiated task is for connecting a project or connection integration.

Resolved invocation shape: support a structured native Codex planning form such as target:connection source_project:<slug-or-path>, while also accepting clear natural-language requests that explicitly say the task is for connecting or integrating a project's skills into the foundry.

Resolved natural-language completion behavior: when the operator asks naturally to connect a project, native Codex planning should keep asking one missing-field question at a time until the complete structured command can be formed.
Resolved minimum command fields: a complete connection initiation requires project:workflow-foundry, target:connection, source_project:<path-or-slug>, and a connection title.

Resolved initial read behavior: unlike plain task initiation, a target:connection initiation should create the tracked task and immediately run the initial read-only source project scan because connection tasks need specialized connection context up front.

Resolved initial scan scope: read the full source project context, including project instructions, project/tracker files, all related task JSON, needed project artifacts, all discovered skill paths and metadata, and isolation risks so the foundry understands the whole connected project before comparison.

Borrowed audit-foundry pattern for connection scans: the initial source scan should be read-only, write a durable Markdown report first, then summarize in chat with loaded sources, findings, recommended next tracker actions, and explicit boundaries.

Resolved location-question policy: default storage and path decisions to repo best-practice placements unless the location affects behavior, safety, isolation, or the operator contract. Use projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/connections/<source-project-slug>-scan.md for initial connection scan reports by default.

Refined write boundary: connected skills may write the normal domain artifacts they are designed to create or maintain, such as real-life workflow packets, but must not mutate source project agent/control-plane files, skill definitions, AGENTS.md, tracker state, or connection records unless a later tracked task explicitly approves that.

Resolved write-zone discovery: the initial connection scan should infer and record allowed write zones for each connected project's normal domain artifacts, protect control-plane paths by default, and ask the operator only when the boundary is ambiguous.

Major model pivot during planning: the operator clarified that the foundry should not primarily directly integrate or run another project's skills as imported skills. Instead, native Codex planning should be able to recognize that a requested task needs a known project workflow capability, such as real-life-workflows, and route/use that workflow through normal task initiation while keeping the source project isolated.

Expanded routing model beyond foundry-to-project: a domain project such as health may need to use another project capability such as real-life-workflows to discover or create workflow packets, so the foundry needs a mechanism to broker or record project-to-project workflow capability relationships.

Refined core scope: this task is about editing how native Codex planning creates tasks so it can create tandem linked tasks across projects, for example a health-domain task that requires a workflow capability from real-life-workflows.

Corrected task model: do not create one task per project by default. Create one unified primary project task, and make part of that task the ability to call standalone workflow capabilities from another project, such as real-life-workflows, during execution.

Resolved task model addition: add capability_dependencies to task JSON so one primary task can explicitly record external project workflow capabilities it is allowed to call, such as real-life-workflows workflow discovery or packet creation.

Resolved dependency finalization gate: native Codex planning may infer capability_dependencies from natural language, but must ask the operator to confirm them before task creation. Once a dependency is named or inferred, it must fully read the referenced source workflow or skill context before proceeding so the task starts from shared understanding.

Resolved task draft gate: after confirmed capability dependencies are loaded, native Codex planning should show a final task draft with primary project, title, summary, capability dependencies, loaded source workflow context, allowed writes, protected paths, and acceptance criteria, then wait for explicit create approval before writing task JSON.

Resolved created phase and discovery behavior: capability-dependent tasks are still created at intake, not planning. During intake drafting, native Codex planning can query known workflows and suggest capability_dependencies when the operator is unsure which workflow capability should be used.

Resolved capability suggestion source: native Codex planning should suggest dependencies only from known registered project trackers and their recorded workflow capabilities, not from broad live scans of arbitrary project folders.

Refined capability discovery and artifact provenance: projects should not have to advertise themselves as reusable capabilities. native Codex planning should infer possible capability dependencies from known tracker context, and the durable requirement is that files created through dependency use are stored and indexed with clear provenance linking the primary task, dependency project, source workflow or skill, and generated artifacts.

Resolved dependency artifact ownership: files created through a dependency call are owned by the primary project task as one of its steps, while retaining provenance links to the dependency project, source workflow or skill, generated files, and protected boundaries.

Resolved dependency step modeling: task JSON should include explicit dependency_steps so each external capability call is represented as an ordered step in the primary task with purpose, dependency project, source workflow or skill, expected inputs and outputs, allowed writes, protected paths, and provenance requirements.

Resolved dependency timing: native Codex planning should front-load dependency discovery during intake drafting and planning by reading or calling relevant known skills/workflows enough to understand available data and step shape. If a new dependency is discovered mid-execution, native Codex planning may propose and add an explicit dependency_step only after loading its workflow context, updating provenance and write boundaries, and receiving operator approval.

Resolved pre-execution dependency artifacts: dependency calls during intake or planning may create real artifacts when needed, but those artifacts must be labeled with intake/planning phase provenance, primary task ownership, dependency project, source workflow or skill, purpose, generated files, and protected boundary metadata so they are not mistaken for normal execution outputs.

Resolved artifact promotion: intake or planning dependency artifacts may be promoted into final task artifacts when the primary task explicitly accepts them, preserving original dependency provenance and adding promotion metadata instead of recreating redundant outputs.

Resolved promotion trigger: when a post-planning PRD names an intake or planning dependency artifact as part of the task, that artifact becomes an official task artifact with original dependency provenance carried forward and promotion metadata added.

Resolved non-promoted artifact handling: intake or planning dependency artifacts not named in the post-planning PRD remain linked evidence or context for the task, but are not treated as final deliverables.

Resolved artifact indexing boundary: dependency-created artifacts are indexed under the primary project task only by default, while the dependency project is referenced through provenance metadata and its tracker is not mutated with backlinks unless a later explicitly approved tracker task allows it.

Resolved dependency preload scope: when a dependency project is selected, native Codex planning should load as much known dependency context as possible, including all known skills or skill metadata, active tracker context, relevant artifacts, and inter-skill relationships, instead of loading only the single skill that initially seems relevant.

Narrowed dependency preload scope: for a selected dependency project, native Codex planning should load all usable known skills or skill metadata and enough relationship/context notes to understand how they can be called, but it does not need to audit or load the entire workflow agent.

Resolved task draft dependency skill map: task drafts with capability dependencies should include a compact map of loaded usable dependency skills, intended purpose in the primary task, call boundary, expected artifacts, allowed writes, and protected paths.
Resolved dependency skill map scope: the task draft should list only dependency skills selected for the task; loaded-but-not-selected skills remain outside the approved call surface.

Resolved helper skill calls: selected dependency skills may call documented helper skills implicitly when those helpers are part of the selected skill's normal workflow, but helper usage must be recorded in provenance after use.

Resolved write-boundary failure behavior: if a selected dependency skill or helper attempts to write outside the approved artifact boundary, execution stops and asks for operator approval. PRD and issue generation should resolve write zones up front so implementation can proceed continuously without avoidable boundary interruptions.

Resolved implementation readiness gate: before implementation starts, PRD or issue artifacts must include a dependency_write_plan for every dependency_step with expected output paths or patterns, allowed write zones, protected paths, artifact promotion rules, provenance requirements, and stop conditions.

Resolved dependency implementation preflight: implementation must verify every dependency_step has an approved dependency_write_plan and block dependency execution when any required plan is missing or incomplete.

Resolved dependency provenance minimum: after a dependency_step runs, record primary project, primary task id, dependency step id, dependency project, selected skill, helper skills used, inputs, generated artifacts, dependency_write_plan used, phase, timestamp, and artifact status such as promoted official artifact or retained evidence/context.

Wrote the PRD for native Codex planning capability dependencies, moved the task to planning, set issues as the next action, and treated direct connection registry or wrapper behavior as out of scope unless needed for dependency provenance.

Published five local issue artifacts for capability task drafts, dependency artifact provenance, dependency write-plan gates, continue-time dependency additions, and validation/regression coverage. Moved the task to work-item planning with implement as the next action but without approving implementation artifacts yet.
Moved to implement after user approved building all issue slices, and approved native Codex planning, native Codex planning, and validate-workflow-state artifacts for implementation.

Implemented capability dependency workflow instructions in native Codex planning and native Codex planning, added workflow-state validation for optional capability dependency, dependency step, write plan, dependency artifact, and provenance fields, completed all five issue checklists, and moved to code-review.

Completed code-review, fixed a validator gap so dependency_steps.selected_skill must match the referenced capability_dependencies selected skill map, wrote the code-review artifact, and reran workflow validation.
Closed workflow-foundry-012 as done after validation passed and the capability dependency task flow was committed locally.
